column2: Phase 2 substrate scaffold authored as worker/src/runtime/audit-gate.ts + 3 supporting files
column3: Phase 2 substrate scaffold authored as worker/src/runtime/audit-gate.ts (AuditGateDO class), worker/wrangler.toml (AUDIT_GATE binding + v4 migration with new_sqlite_classes), worker/src/index.ts (POST /audit-gate-test route + handleAuditGateTest forwarder keyed by head_sha), worker/src/types.ts (AUDIT_GATE: DurableObjectNamespace). DO extends raw DurableObject matching ConversationDO pattern (NOT Agents SDK base — Agents SDK features hibernation-aware WebSocket, sub-agent facets, durable fibers are not load-bearing for a one-shot validator). Implements 5 runtime-contract responsibilities: profile resolution via GitHub raw fetch + hand-rolled YAML parser (tested against real persona.md, 9 sanity checks pass), system-prompt assembly fetching the AMS constraint, validator-role enforcement (structural; mutating-tool filter is Phase 3), one-shot session with STUBBED Anthropic inference returning synthetic verdict that exercises post-processing, output-contract validation parsing final fenced JSON block. Inference stubbed by intent — Phase 3 wires real Anthropic+oddkit MCP alongside side-by-side validation harness. Phase 2 proves substrate; Phase 3 proves parity.

column2: In ODD, the operator reviews plans/docs/governance, not code
column3: In ODD, the operator reviews plans/docs/governance, not code. The Phase 1 memory rule (#3 v1) was over-broad — "surface all artifact files before commit/PR" externalized code-level decisions onto operator attention, which violates the bottleneck principle the rule was meant to honor. Refined v2: surface canon docs, journal TSVs (standalone), proposals, migration plans, and governance artifacts; code/config/bundled-journals go straight to PR. Operator caught the over-breadth when I surfaced 4 code files: "Sorry, in ODD I don't review code. Just plans and docs and governance." The corrected rule is more useful and more honest about role separation: operator is gate-holder at the outcome level; agent makes implementation calls.

column2: AuditGateDO uses raw DurableObject base, not Agents SDK Agent base, for one-shot validator sessions
column3: For audit-gate substrate composition, raw DurableObject is the chosen base class for AuditGateDO over the Agents SDK Agent base. Rationale: AuditGateDO is an MCP CLIENT (consumes oddkit MCP), not an MCP SERVER (which is what AmsMcpAgent serves via McpAgent extends Agent). The Agents SDK base offers persistent identity, hibernation-aware WebSocket, sub-agent facets via DO Facets, scheduled alarms, and durable execution fibers — none of which a one-shot validator session needs. The migration plan's "DO + Agents SDK + Project Think" composition target applies to subscribed sessions or multi-step workflows; the audit-gate's one_shot session_type per the runtime-contract Composition Rules table makes raw DurableObject the lower-overhead correct fit. If Phase 3 reveals the Anthropic+MCP integration is easier via the Agents SDK base, this is reversible via a refactor PR (the class name and binding name stay; the parent class swaps).

column2: Hand-rolled YAML mini-parser is scope-limited to the persona-profile shape; schema additions break it
column3: Hand-rolled YAML mini-parser in audit-gate.ts handles top-level scalars, nested objects at 2-space indent, inline string arrays, and block arrays — sufficient for the persona-profile shape per klappy://canon/methods/persona-shaped-agent-runtime §The Persona Profile but explicitly NOT a general YAML implementation. Tested against the real merged persona.md (PR #79 head sha 94970dd1) with 9 sanity checks: persona name, version (number type), system_prompt_uri, role, mcp_servers.operational, mcp_servers.task_relevant (empty array), knowledge_bases (block array), surface_profiles.audit nested fields, brand_discipline (null). All passed. Failure mode: persona schema additions (multi-line strings, anchors, deep nesting) break the parser. Mitigation: Phase 3 may swap to js-yaml dep or move to a JSON profile sibling; for Phase 2 the parser is documented as scope-limited.

column2: /audit-gate-test endpoint is wide-open in production deploys; Phase 3 MUST add auth before unstubbing
column3: /audit-gate-test endpoint is wide-open in production deploys to ams.klappy.dev and ams.truthkit.ai. Phase 2 stub returns synthetic data so cost-of-abuse is bounded (DO instantiation + small DO duration billing, no Anthropic key consumed yet). Phase 3 MUST add auth before unstubbing inference, or the endpoint becomes a key-burning vector. Proposed Phase 3 auth: shared-secret header (worker secret) or HMAC of request body. Until Phase 3 lands, the wide-open route is acceptable because the stub does not invoke billable inference. Tracked as Phase 3 prerequisite.

column2: Phase 2 PR forthcoming; audit gate will self-review the code that will host its own future session
column3: Phase 2 PR forthcoming at klappy/agent-messaging-service. Four files: canon-code, no canon docs in this PR. Audit gate will auto-fire on the PR; expected interaction is the gate auditing the new code that the gate is being migrated to (deeper self-reference than Phase 1 — the gate's spawned-agent-session reviews the worker code that will eventually host the gate's own spawned-agent-session). Gauntlet pre-PR: oddkit_time first, oddkit_preflight before drafting, oddkit_challenge mode=execution surfaced 5 substantive concerns (DO migration reversibility, endpoint security, YAML parser fragility, base class choice, stubbed inference) — all addressed via this DOLCHEO trail rather than file edits since they're decisions not bugs. block_until_addressed: true was satisfied by encoding the decisions explicitly. Phase 3 next on operator signal: side-by-side validation harness, real Anthropic+oddkit MCP wiring, endpoint auth, two-three real PRs through the gate. ~1 hour wiring + operator-paced validation.

column2: Artifact-producing execution steps must surface the drafted file in-chat before commit/PR
column3: Artifact-producing execution steps must surface the drafted file in-chat (via present_files) for operator review BEFORE committing to a branch or opening a PR. Phase 1 (PR #79) went directly draft → branch → PR PUT → PR open without an intermediate operator-review surface. Operator caught it post-merge: "I should have asked you to surface the file for my viewing and review." The rule: any local-disk artifact destined for a PR must be surfaced via present_files before the commit step. Applies to canon docs, journal TSVs, code files, config — anything that becomes a PR. Orthogonal to the gauntlet (preflight/challenge/encode/validate); sits between encoding and committing.
